meta.siteTitle: Kris Swodeck – Front-End Web Developer / Software Engineer
meta.description: Portfolio of Kris Swodeck, a Front-End Web Developer / Software Engineer with 7+ years building and testing production web applications. Specializing in JavaScript, TypeScript, and the Vue / Nuxt ecosystem with deep QA and test-automation experience.

profile.name: Kris Swodeck
profile.title: Front-End Web Developer / Software Engineer
profile.tagline: JavaScript • TypeScript • Front-End Development & Engineering • Responsive, Accessible Web Apps
taglines: 7+ years building and testing production web apps

summary: Front-End Web Developer / Software Engineer with 7+ years building and testing performant, accessible production web applications across the full software development lifecycle. Specialized in JavaScript, TypeScript, HTML5, CSS3, and the Vue / Nuxt.js ecosystem, with a solid Node.js foundation. My dual background in development and quality engineering (Selenium, Mocha, Chai test automation) means I write reliable, well-tested, maintainable code — but what sets me apart is how I think about the work: business coursework trained me to weigh the user, the customer, and the business impact behind every feature. I don't just build what's specced — I ask whether it solves the right problem. And when something's broken, I have an unquenchable urge to fix it.

roles.title: Web Developer
bullets: Built and maintained a customer-facing EdTech platform in Vue, Nuxt.js, and Node.js that enabled PK-12 administrators and educators to monitor and manage students across an internet-based math curriculum.
Translated product requirements into responsive, accessible UI with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, delivering a consistent experience across devices and screen sizes.
Developed and shipped customer-facing features in Vue and Nuxt.js, improving the usability and overall experience of the platform.
Partnered with QA to triage and resolve defects before release, contributing to stable deployments and a seamless user experience.
Continuously evaluated emerging front-end technologies and industry best practices, integrating improvements to enhance development workflows and platform reliability.

roles.title: Quality Engineer
bullets: Supported the SDLC of learning-based math games using Agile / Scrum QA methodologies.
Built and maintained automated UI test suites in Selenium with JavaScript (Node.js) to catch regressions before release.
Integrated Mocha, Chai, and BrowserStack into the test pipeline to enable cross-browser automated testing.
Isolated, reproduced, and tracked defects through resolution, verifying fixes against product requirements.
Authored detailed, reproducible bug reports in Jira (with Git, Bitbucket, and Confluence) to speed developer turnaround.
Wrote and ran SQL queries to validate data accuracy across game software products before release.

roles.title: Quality Assurance Intern
bullets: Developed and executed automated test scripts that streamlined regression testing and reduced manual test effort.
Conducted risk assessments and tracked quality metrics across web products to flag reliability issues before launch.
Reproduced customer-reported issues and partnered with developers to verify patches, improving diagnosis accuracy and fix turnaround.

roles.title: Web Designer
bullets: Designed and maintained the company website in WordPress using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
Implemented SEO/SEM and social media strategies to grow the company's organic traffic and digital presence.
Applied UX/UI design principles to improve navigation, accessibility, and visual hierarchy; analyzed web-traffic data in Google Analytics to inform design decisions and measure impact.

projects.name: CalcCrunch
bullets: Designed, built, and launched CalcCrunch.com, a free platform of 100+ calculators across several categories (financial, real estate, business, sales), engineered with TypeScript, JavaScript, HTML, and CSS.
Built mobile-first, registration-free tools with no paywalls or intrusive ads, prioritizing user experience and privacy.

projects.name: This Portfolio (Multi-Framework)
bullets: Single portfolio rendered in four front-end implementations – Vanilla JS/TS, React, Vue, and Angular – each served as its own independently built app at a dedicated URL on the same subdomain (e.g. kswodeck.swodecksitesolutions.com/angular/).
All four implementations render from a single shared content source, demonstrating cross-framework breadth and a consistent component architecture.

projects.name: Travel Beyond Boundaries
bullets: Built a content-driven travel blog with user-friendly navigation aimed at family, luxury, and budget travel audiences.

projects.name: Real Estate Smart Choice
bullets: Built a real estate content site with WordPress, JavaScript, HTML, and CSS, and grew visibility through SEO and social strategies.

education.credential: B.S., Computer Science; Minor, Business Administration

aboutSite.intro: This portfolio is engineered as a live demonstration of cross-framework front-end proficiency. The same content, design, and user experience is delivered through four completely independent apps — each built natively in its own framework, each served at its own URL.

pillars.title: Four Frameworks, One Design
pillars.description: Vanilla JS/TS, Angular, React, and Vue each independently implement the full portfolio — same content, same design, zero shared runtime code. A live proof that the same UI challenge can be solved natively in any major front-end framework.

pillars.title: Single Source of Truth
pillars.description: All resume content lives in one shared/content.json file. Every framework reads from it at build time and in development — edit once, update everywhere. No duplicated data, no versions drifting apart.

pillars.title: Shared TypeScript Layer
pillars.description: A central shared/types.ts defines every data interface once. Theme detection and content-fetching utilities are also shared — one implementation imported by all four apps. Each framework re-exports what it needs without duplicating logic.

pillars.title: Parallel Build Pipeline
pillars.description: A custom Node.js build script spawns all four framework builds simultaneously using parallel child processes, then assembles a single deployable dist/ folder — faster than any sequential approach.

pillars.title: Live Dev Server Middleware
pillars.description: Each Vite dev server intercepts content.json requests via a custom plugin and serves the file live from shared/ — content changes reflect instantly in the browser without any manual file-copy step.

pillars.title: Automated Static Deployment
pillars.description: Every push to master triggers a Netlify build: install dependencies, run the parallel build, publish the output. Automatic HTTPS, global CDN, sub-5-minute deploys — no server to manage.
